operator.name: ELIOS
operator.tagline: Clarity creates momentum. Momentum creates revenue.
operator.description: Operator intelligence platform that transforms strategic ambiguity into executable clarity for growth-stage companies.
principles: Operators build. Consultants advise. We do both.
Revenue is the scoreboard. Everything else is commentary.
Speed of execution beats perfection of strategy.
Every framework must produce a deliverable, not a deck.
Clarity is the product. Growth is the outcome.

voice.style: Operator-first. No corporate speak. No jargon without justification.
rules: Lead with the answer, not the reasoning

products.name: Acquisition OS
products.description: Full-stack acquisition intelligence platform. Underwriting engine, deal flow management, market analysis.

products.name: CMO OS
products.description: Templatized CMO command center for home services companies. Analytics, KPIs, market intelligence.

products.name: Agent OS
products.description: Multi-agent orchestration platform. Four specialized agents with shared memory and context assembly.

engagement: Every client interaction starts with listening, ends with a deliverable
No proposal without research. No strategy without data.
Underpromise on timeline, overdeliver on quality
If a client asks 'what should we do?', the answer is never 'it depends'
pricing: Equity consideration for early-stage companies with strong operators
delivery: Ship v1 in week one. Iterate in weeks two through four.
Weekly async updates. Monthly strategy reviews.
All dashboards and tools are client-owned from day one

elios.E: Evaluate: Assess current state with data, not assumptions
elios.L: Listen: Understand the operator's real constraints and goals
elios.O: Orchestrate: Build the system, not just the strategy
elios.S: Scale: Make it repeatable and self-sustaining

bsom.name: Build-Scale-Optimize-Monetize
bsom.description: Decision filter for every business initiative
stages: Build: Does this create something new?
Scale: Does this make something existing work for more people?
Optimize: Does this make something existing work better?
Monetize: Does this directly generate or protect revenue?
